title: Rotated Sorted Array with Duplicate Elements and Edge Cases

statement.markdown: In this problem, you are given a rotated sorted array with duplicate elements. You need to search for an element in the array. There are some edge cases that need to be handled, like searching for elements that are outside the bounds of the array and arrays with all elements equal.
statement.inputFormat: The input will contain an array of integers (nums) and a target integer (target).
statement.outputFormat: Your function should return the index of the target in the array if it exists. If the target does not exist, you should return -1.
statement.notes: You should handle edge cases like searching for elements that are outside the bounds of the array and arrays with all elements equal.

examples.explanation: The target 7 exists in the array at index 7.
The target 3 exists in the array at index 0.
The target 20 does not exist in the array.

editorial.title: Editorial title
editorial.betterSolution: Explanation of a better solution approach

functionSignature.python: def search(self, nums: list[int], target: int) -> int:
